meaning: adj.有斑点的,有条纹的,容易变的
examples: The wallpaper was streaky with grease.
She has streaky fair hair and blue eyes.
"Avoid the streaky cheek-to-ears look of the '80s, " she says.
The aim is to freeze the runners, but at the same time blur the background with a streaky effect to show speed.
"Although it might be very natural looking and it's not orange or streaky, no-one in their right mind would have sunbathed to achieve it, " she says.
